notes: Hand-authored workflow case grounded in the public PyPSA unit commitment example.
The case focuses on workflow stages rather than reproducing a full PyPSA model.
Rolling horizon remains a classical control step in this prototype.

component.scalability_note: PlanQK README states QAOA via Qiskit runtime is extremely limited in problem size; treat as exploratory.

blocking_conditions: Scalability or integration warnings require fallback and keep the component in exploratory status.
positive_conditions: Target subproblem unit_commitment_binary_core is compatible with the case.
rationale: Component is admissible only for exploratory assessment-guided partitioning.
Exploratory partition allows only the discrete commitment core to be quantum-enabled.
Dispatch, feasibility repair, rolling horizon control, and postprocessing remain classical.
This is a limited exploratory partition, not a deployment recommendation.
Component is admissible only for exploratory assessment-guided partitioning.

warning: This is assessment guidance, not solver performance.
